# Context Package YAML Schema

Read this during Step 8. Create a YAML structure at
`contexts/<FEATURE_SLUG>_<TASK_TYPE>_<YYYYMMDD>.yaml` matching this schema exactly:

```yaml
context_package:
  id: <feature-slug>_<task-type>_<YYYYMMDD>
  task: <user's task description>
  scope:
    feature: <FEATURE_TERM>
    task_type: <TASK_TYPE>
    new_or_existing: <new|existing>
    out_of_scope: <user's answer>
  aspects:                          # from Step 1.5 — one entry per ASPECTS member
    - aspect_id: <aspect.aspect_id>  # stable join key for context_items[]/
                                      # gaps_detected[] — see D17
      name: <aspect.name>
      what_l3_covered: <true|false>    # from l3_coverage (Step 4)
      what_l2_covered: <true|false>    # from l2_coverage (Step 5)
      what_l1_covered: <true|false>    # true if Step 7.1 found a What-L1 item for
                                        # this aspect, incl. citation-following
      llm_knowledge_used: <true|false> # true if this aspect's Q3 (Step 7.1 step 5a)
                                        # offer was accepted
      web_fallback_used: <true|false>  # true if this aspect's web fallback
                                        # (Step 7.1 step 5a, D18) offer was accepted
  generated_at: <ISO timestamp>
  human_approved: false
  content_hash: <hash8>             # see "content_hash maintenance" below
                                     # (D19 v2, C1/C2) — always present
  supersedes: <old-package-id>      # only present if this package was created
                                     # via Step 3's "regenerate" branch for an
                                     # existing same-feature/same-task_type
                                     # package (D19 v2, C2) — otherwise omit
                                     # this field entirely
  what_l1_fallback_count: 0        # incremented by Step 7.1 for each What-L1 item
                                    # added; 0 if what_l1.enabled: false or none found
  llm_knowledge_count: 0            # incremented by Step 7.1 step 5a (Q3) for each
                                     # accepted training-knowledge item — distinct from
                                     # llm_scaffold_count (Step 7.2's whole-aspect scaffolds)
  web_fallback_count: 0             # incremented by Step 7.1 step 5a (D18) for each
                                     # accepted web-fallback item — only nonzero if
                                     # what_l1.allow_web_fallback: true
  how_l1_fallback_count: 0          # incremented by Step 2.1 for each How-L1 item
                                     # added, incl. citation-following; 0 if
                                     # how_l1.enabled: false or none found
  conflicts_count: <N>
  llm_scaffold_count: <N>          # items generated by LLM due to complete gap
  org_convention_ref: org-conventions/<TASK_TYPE>.yaml
  how_l1_covered: false             # true if Step 2.1 found a How-L1 item for this
                                     # package — package-level, not per-aspect
                                     # (How-L1 is task-type-scoped, not aspect-scoped)

  summary:
    - "<bullet 1 — key What-L3 finding>"
    - "<bullet 2 — key What-L2 finding>"
    - "<bullet 3 — any conflict or gap>"
    - "<bullet 4 — any notable open question>"
    - "<bullet 5 — any LLM-suggested item>"

  context_items:
    - id: ctx_<NNN>
      layer: what-l3                 # or what-l2, what-l1, llm-generated
      source: <file:line or doc-section>
      type: implementation           # or requirement, domain-spec, llm-generated
      confidence: EXTRACTED          # or INFERRED, SUGGESTED
      aspect_id: <aspect.aspect_id>  # which ASPECTS member this item addresses —
                                      # join key, stable even if aspect.name is
                                      # edited later (D17)
      aspect: <aspect.name>          # display name, for human-readable audit
      summary: >
        <concise description of what this item says>
      what_l1_fallback: false

    - id: ctx_<NNN>
      layer: constraints              # third dimension — see D11. Absent if no
                                       # COMPILED-GUIDELINES.md (optional infrastructure).
      source: "starter_kit/project_guidelines/COMPILED-GUIDELINES.md (<Global|scope label>)"
      type: constraint
      constraint_class: compliance | convention | scheduling
      scope: <Global | path-glob>
      confidence: EXTRACTED
      summary: >
        <the guidance, faithfully paraphrased>

    - id: ctx_<NNN>
      layer: what-l1                  # external reference — see Step 7.1, D2/D13/D14.
                                       # Absent if what_l1.enabled: false or no
                                       # both-layers-gap aspect was closed by it.
      source: "<what_l1.path>/<file>.md (<clause_id or heading title>)"
      type: domain-spec
      confidence: EXTRACTED
      aspect_id: <aspect.aspect_id>
      aspect: <aspect.name>
      summary: >
        <the external guidance, faithfully paraphrased — note that product
        applicability has not been confirmed>. <If found via citation-following
        (D14): "Found via citation-following from <source clause/section>.">
      what_l1_fallback: true

    - id: ctx_<NNN>
      layer: what-l1                  # Q3 training-knowledge item — see Step 7.1
                                       # step 5a. Absent unless the user accepted
                                       # such an offer for some aspect.
      source: "llm_training_knowledge (<spec/standard name if identifiable, else
        'general domain knowledge'>)"
      type: domain-spec
      confidence: SUGGESTED
      knowledge_cutoff: <model knowledge-cutoff date, e.g. 2025-08>
      aspect_id: <aspect.aspect_id>
      aspect: <aspect.name>
      summary: >
        <the training-knowledge content, including the unverified-against-current-spec
        caveat from the Step 7.1 step 5a prompt>
      what_l1_fallback: true

    - id: ctx_<NNN>
      layer: what-l1                  # web fallback item — see Step 7.1 step 5a
                                       # (D18). Absent unless
                                       # what_l1.allow_web_fallback: true and the
                                       # user accepted such an offer for some aspect.
      source: "web_search(<query>) retrieved_at: <ISO timestamp>"
      type: domain-spec
      confidence: SUGGESTED
      aspect_id: <aspect.aspect_id>
      aspect: <aspect.name>
      summary: >
        <the web result content, including the unverified-against-project
        caveat from the Step 7.1 step 5a prompt>
      what_l1_fallback: true

    - id: ctx_<NNN>
      layer: how-l1                   # org-wide process standard — see Step 2.1.
                                       # Absent if how_l1.enabled: false or How-L2
                                       # already covered the task type. No
                                       # aspect_id/aspect — task-type-scoped, not
                                       # aspect-scoped (constraints-shaped, not
                                       # what-l1-shaped).
      source: "<file path> (<clause_id or title>)"
      type: process-standard
      confidence: EXTRACTED
      summary: >
        <the process-standard guidance, faithfully paraphrased — note that
        org-QMS applicability has not been confirmed>
      how_l1_fallback: true

  conflicts_detected:               # empty list if none
    - id: conflict_<NNN>
      conflict_type: l2-l3-contradiction  # or constraint-lateral, constraint-vertical
      topic: <topic>
      what_l2_claim: "<claim from requirements>"        # l2-l3-contradiction only
      what_l3_finding: "<finding from code>"            # l2-l3-contradiction only
      scope_a: <path-glob A>                            # constraint-lateral only
      claim_a: "<guidance from scope A>"                # constraint-lateral only
      scope_b: <path-glob B>                            # constraint-lateral only
      claim_b: "<guidance from scope B>"                # constraint-lateral only
      interaction_point: "<symbol from Step 4.5>"       # constraint-lateral only
      interpretation_options:                           # l2-l3-contradiction only
        - "<option 1>"
        - "<option 2>"
      resolution_required: true
      note: "<why this matters for the artifact>"

  gaps_detected:                    # empty list if none
    - aspect_id: <aspect.aspect_id>  # join key — see D17
      aspect: <aspect.name>          # display name
      layers_checked: [what-l2, what-l3, what-l1]   # what-l1 only if Step 7.1 ran
      what_l1_fallback_used: false   # true if Step 7.1 found a relevant What-L1
                                      # item for this aspect, including items found
                                      # via citation-following (D14) — see Step 7.1
      llm_knowledge_used: false      # true if this aspect's Q3 (Step 7.1 step 5a)
                                      # training-knowledge offer was accepted
      web_fallback_used: false       # true if this aspect's web fallback
                                      # (Step 7.1 step 5a, D18) offer was accepted
      note: "<what is missing>"

  open_questions: []              # empty after Step 7.5 resolves all

  decisions_log:
    - topic: "<question topic>"
      decided: "<resolved value>"

  non_regression_risks:           # populated by Step 4.5 blast radius analysis
    - "<symbol being changed>: <what must remain stable for dependents not to regress>"

  # last_enriched_at and domain_additions_count are added by consumer skills
  # if domain items are approved during artifact-level enrichment (Step 4.5 of consumer skills)
```

Aim for 5–12 `context_items`. More than 15 makes the package unwieldy to review.

Also create a companion markdown summary at
`contexts/<FEATURE_SLUG>_<TASK_TYPE>_<YYYYMMDD>.md` with a human-readable version
(the `summary` bullets + the conflicts listed plainly).

**`content_hash` maintenance (two-pass save, D19 v2 C1/C2):** any time
`contexts/<id>.yaml` is written or rewritten — this step's initial save (on
approval), Step 3's fold-addenda re-save, or a consumer skill's domain-enrichment
write-back (per `CONSUMING-CONTEXT-PACKAGE.md`) — save in two passes:
1. Write the YAML with `content_hash` omitted (first save) or left at its
   prior, now-stale value.
2. Run `python scripts/content_hash.py contexts/<id>.yaml` to compute the
   current `<hash8>`.
3. Patch `content_hash: <hash8>` into the file and re-save. (`content_hash.py`
   excludes its own `content_hash:` line from the hash it computes, so this
   patch does not invalidate the hash just computed — it's a fixed point.)

This keeps `content_hash` an accurate fingerprint of the package's current
content for `CONSUMING-CONTEXT-PACKAGE.md`'s drift check.

Return to `SKILL.md` Step 9 once the package is assembled and saved.
